---
title: "Project template — copy me"
description: "One-line hook that appears on the project card and in social previews."
author: "Malte Nalenz"
date: "2026-06-01"
categories: [template, forecasting]
image: thumbnail.png
image-alt: "Thumbnail shown on the projects listing card"
draft: false
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
    fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

A short lead paragraph (two or three sentences) summarising the problem, the
approach, and the outcome. This sits above the first heading and appears as
the listing preview when the `description` field is empty.

## Problem

What was the situation, who cared about it, and what made it hard? Keep this
concrete: the dataset size, the constraint that ruled out the obvious
approach, the business or research goal.

## Approach

What you actually did. Include the modelling choices, the alternatives you
considered, and any non-obvious engineering work. Diagrams and plots are
welcome — see the [blog post template](../../../blog/posts/template/index.qmd)
for examples of Mermaid, matplotlib, and callouts.

## Outcome

Numbers where possible: error reduction, latency, cost savings, adoption,
papers, repo stars. If the project is ongoing, say what the current state is
and what is next.

## Stack

Python, pandas, scikit-learn, …

## Links

- [GitHub repo](https://github.com/)
- [Write-up / paper](#)
- [Demo](#)

---

### How to use this template

1. Copy this folder: `cp -r projects/items/template projects/items/<your-slug>`
2. Edit the frontmatter at the top (title, description, date, categories, image).
3. Set `draft: false` when ready to publish.
4. Drop a `thumbnail.png` (square or 4:3 works well) into the same folder.
